days.title: Neuter -то: places in town

vocab.exampleEn: The café is next to the park.
The cinema is in the centre.

grammar.title: The neuter definite article -то
grammar.explanation: Bulgarian marks 'the' with a suffix, not a separate word. Neuter nouns (most ending in -о or -е) add -то to say 'the': село → селото (the village), кафене → кафенето (the café), място → мястото (the place). The article goes on the END of the noun. Bulgarian has no cases, so the word never changes its ending for grammar reasons — only this article suffix is added.

reading.title: В града
reading.text: Това е кафене. Кафенето е тук. Училището е там.
reading.translation: This is a café. The café is here. The school is there.

days.speakingPrompt: Point at three neuter places (a café, a school, a cinema) and say where each one is, using -то and the words тук/там.

days.title: Neuter article with 'is/are' (съм)

grammar.title: Neuter -то with the verb 'to be'
grammar.explanation: Unlike Russian, Bulgarian DOES use a present-tense verb 'to be': съм, си, е, сме, сте, са. For a single neuter thing you use е (is): Летището е далече (The airport is far). Neuter adjectives end in -о and agree with the noun: ново кафене / кафенето е ново (new café / the café is new), старо село (old village). Remember the -то article still goes only on the noun (and on a front adjective, which you will meet later).

reading.title: Близо или далече?
reading.text: Киното е близо. Летището е далече. Кафенето е ново и хубаво.
reading.translation: The cinema is near. The airport is far. The café is new and nice.

days.speakingPrompt: Describe four neuter places near you using е plus an adjective (нов/стар/хубав) and близо/далече, e.g. 'Кафенето е ново и близо.'

days.title: Plural article -те: the places

grammar.title: The plural definite article -те
grammar.explanation: Once a noun is already in the plural, you make it definite ('the …s') by adding -те to the plural form: магазини → магазините (the shops), улици → улиците (the streets), хотели → хотелите (the hotels). The verb 'to be' for plurals is са (are): Магазините са затворени (The shops are closed). Adjectives in the plural end in -и: нови хотели, затворени магазини.

reading.title: Магазините
reading.text: Тук има много магазини. Магазините са отворени. Улиците са хубави.
reading.translation: There are many shops here. The shops are open. The streets are nice.

days.speakingPrompt: Say that the shops, the streets and the hotels in your town are open/closed/new, using plural -те and the verb са.

days.title: Forming plurals: masculine & feminine nouns

grammar.title: Forming the plural of nouns
grammar.explanation: Bulgarian plurals depend on gender. Feminine nouns in -а/-я usually change to -и: улица → улици, банка → банки, аптека → аптеки. Many one-syllable masculine nouns add -ове: град → градове, парк → паркове. Longer masculine nouns usually add -и: магазин → магазини, хотел → хотели. (Neuter plurals come tomorrow.) Add -те afterwards to make them definite: градовете, банките.

reading.title: Градове и паркове
reading.text: Градовете са големи. Парковете са хубави. Аптеките са отворени.
reading.translation: The cities are big. The parks are nice. The pharmacies are open.

days.speakingPrompt: Take four singular nouns (град, парк, банка, аптека), say each plural form, then add -те. Describe them as big/small/open/closed.

days.title: Forming plurals: neuter nouns

vocab.exampleEn: The boys are in the park.
The girls are at the school.

grammar.title: Neuter plurals and their article
grammar.explanation: Neuter nouns in -о usually take -а in the plural: село → села, езеро → езера, място → места. Neuter nouns in -е often take -та or -я: момче → момчета, момиче → момичета, кафене → кафенета. To make a neuter plural definite, add -та: селата (the villages), местата (the places), момчетата (the boys). Note the article here is -та (not -те) because the plural ends in a vowel — listen for it.

reading.title: Езера и места
reading.text: Езерата са красиви и чисти. Местата са хубави. Момичетата са там.
reading.translation: The lakes are beautiful and clean. The places are nice. The girls are there.

days.speakingPrompt: Make the plural of four neuter nouns (село, езеро, място, момче), add the article (-та), and say one thing about each using красив/чист/хубав.

days.title: Putting it together: my town

vocab.exampleEn: The shops are in the centre.

grammar.title: Singular -то/-ът and plural -те/-та together
grammar.explanation: Now combine everything. Singular places take their gender article (центърът, болницата, кафенето); plural places take -те after a consonant (площадите, сградите) or -та after a vowel-ending neuter plural (селата). Use е for one thing and са for many, and prepositions instead of cases: Болницата е в центъра, Магазините са до площада. Bulgarian never changes the noun for case — only the article and number change.

examples.english: The hospital is in the centre.

reading.title: Моят град
reading.text: Центърът е хубав. Сградите са високи. Парковете са зелени и чисти.
reading.translation: The centre is nice. The buildings are tall. The parks are green and clean.

days.speakingPrompt: Describe your town in five sentences: name singular places with their article (-ът/-та/-то) and plural places with -те/-та, using е and са.

days.title: Review: articles and plurals

grammar.title: Week 6 recap: -то, -те and forming plurals
grammar.explanation: This week: neuter 'the' = -то (село → селото, кафене → кафенето). Plural 'the' = -те after a consonant (магазините, улиците, сградите) and -та on neuter plurals ending in a vowel (селата, местата). Plurals: feminine -а → -и (банка → банки); short masculine + -ове (град → градове); longer masculine + -и (хотел → хотели); neuter -о → -а (езеро → езера), neuter -е → -та (момче → момчета). Use е for one and са for many. Bulgarian has no cases — relations come from word order and prepositions (в, до, на, от).

examples.english: The villages are small and nice.

reading.title: Къде са?
reading.text: — Кафенето отворено ли е? — Да, кафенето е отворено. Магазините са затворени, но пазарът е тук, близо до площада.
reading.translation: — Is the café open? — Yes, the café is open. The shops are closed, but the market is here, near the square.

days.speakingPrompt: Role-play a short dialogue: ask whether two places are open or closed, answer with е/са, and say where one place is using a preposition (в/до/близо до). Use at least one neuter -то noun and one plural -те noun.
